research.product: Microsoft Graph calendarView (Outlook calendar events over a window)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, GET /users/{id}/calendarView (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0)

official_sources.title: List calendarView - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/{id}/calendarView request; REQUIRED startDateTime/endDateTime ISO 8601 query parameters interpreted from their own offset (no offset = UTC) and unaffected by the Prefer timezone header; Prefer: outlook.timezone header controls the zone of start/end in the response with UTC as the default; 200 OK event collection; calendarView returns occurrences, exceptions and single instances expanded over the window

official_sources.title: Paging Microsoft Graph data in your app
official_sources.used_for: @odata.nextLink must be reused as returned until absent; the $skiptoken/$skip inside is opaque and must not be extracted or rebuilt; custom (non-Authorization) request headers are not automatically included in subsequent page requests and must be set explicitly; tokens from retries must not replace the last successful link

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After in seconds and the TooManyRequests error envelope; documented recovery is waiting the Retry-After delay and retrying the same request

research.language_rationale: The export runs from the ops team's PowerShell 7 scheduled-task runner, and the calendarView surface is plain REST with exact header/query semantics; a script module over Invoke-WebRequest pins the wire contract directly, without requiring the Microsoft.Graph PowerShell SDK module on the runner.

verified_facts: calendarView lives at GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{id}/calendarView and REQUIRES startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 form.
Range values are interpreted using the offset inside the value itself (none = UTC) and are explicitly not affected by the Prefer: outlook.timezone header.
Prefer: outlook.timezone="{zone}" selects the time zone of start/end values in the RESPONSE; without it Graph returns UTC.
calendarView returns the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in the window — recurring series arrive pre-expanded as type=occurrence events carrying a seriesMasterId.
Event start/end are objects { dateTime, timeZone } where dateTime carries seven fractional digits.

Responses page via @odata.nextLink; the link must be reused as returned (opaque token), a page may hold zero items, and custom headers such as Prefer must be re-attached explicitly to follow-up page requests.
Throttling returns 429 with a Retry-After header in whole seconds; the documented recovery is to wait that long and repeat the request.
Graph errors use the { error: { code, message } } envelope; 401 InvalidAuthenticationToken and 5xx server failures are distinct from retryable 429s.
